section: H2:Information on the processing of personal data for App Acty > H4:Within the meaning of Art. 13 of Legislative Decree No 196 of 30.6.2003 and Art. 13 of Regulation (EU) 679/2016
sentence: This privacy statement – drafted according to Article 13 of Legislative Decree No 196 of 30 June 2003 (“Privacy Code”) and Art. 13 of Reg. (EU) No. 2016/679 (“GDPR”) – regulates the personal data processing carried out by the Data Controller via the “ACTY” Mobile Phone Application (hereinafter referred to also only as the “APP”); and describes the purposes of and procedures used for the processing of the personal data of the Users who download, install and use the APP.

section: H2:Information on the processing of personal data for App Acty > H4:Within the meaning of Art. 13 of Legislative Decree No 196 of 30.6.2003 and Art. 13 of Regulation (EU) 679/2016
sentence: This privacy statement is provided exclusively for the App, and any data processing carried out by Icona but not for the data processing carried out by third-party customers of ICONA Srl, who provide support through the APP. Each Customer company will provide the User with its privacy statement on personal data processing.

section: H2:Information on the processing of personal data for App Acty > H4:Within the meaning of Art. 13 of Legislative Decree No 196 of 30.6.2003 and Art. 13 of Regulation (EU) 679/2016
sentence: To complete the APP installation, the User must read, understand and accept this Privacy Policy’s contents.

section: H2:Information on the processing of personal data for App Acty > H4:Within the meaning of Art. 13 of Legislative Decree No 196 of 30.6.2003 and Art. 13 of Regulation (EU) 679/2016
sentence: This privacy statement may be subject to updates without prior warning: by downloading, installing, and using the APP, the User fully and automatically accepts the variations.

The “Data Controller” is the company ICONA SRL, represented by its pro tempore legal representative, with a registered office in Cinisello Balsamo (MI) in Viale Brianza 20, Tax Code 01110530191, VAT Number IT02812760961 (hereinafter also referred to for the sake of brevity as “ICONA” or the “Data Controller” or the “Company”).

The “Users” are the natural persons to whom the personal data processed by the Data Controller refer (hereinafter also referred to for the sake of brevity as the “Users” or “Data Subjects”); who download, install and use the APP, developed and created by ICONA for Android and IOS operating systems, which allows the user to use the software named “ACTY”.

The Customer/Licensee is a company or individual who has signed a user license agreement with ICONA Srl for the ACTY software and provides the remote technical support requested by the User via the APP.
“ACTY MOBILE” is the Mobile Application of the remote video support platform created by ICONA.

The APP can be used (i) in “Master” mode by the Customer/Licensee to provide remote support with the credentials obtained by the license agreement for the software and/or (ii) in “slave” mode by the final customer to request remote support.
ICONA provides the APP to interested users through a free user license.

section: H2:Nature of the data processed and user registration
sentence: The User who downloads the APP must register to use the APP and associated services. Registration is free of charge and confidential and can only be performed by adults.

section: H2:Nature of the data processed and user registration
sentence: The User must provide the Data Controller with truthful data and keep them updated, under their sole responsibility.

section: H2:Nature of the data processed and user registration
sentence: By registering, the User authorizes ICONA to process and use their data, conferred at the time of registration, to favor and facilitate the User’s access to the APP, also enabling them not to have to register again each time they enter the APP.

section: H2:Nature of the data processed and user registration
sentence: The User undertakes to keep the information about their Account (User-Id) and the password for accessing the APP confidential and will be the only partly responsible for all activities using their Account.

section: H2:Nature of the data processed and user registration
sentence: The User of the APP can access the service both as a “Registered User” and as an “Anonymous User” via the following procedures:

The personal data communicated by the Users who download, install and use the APP will be processed by the Data Controller for the following purposes: To fulfill the request for remote technical support made by the User via the APP. ICONA will communicate the User’s data to the Customer/Licensee to authorize the latter to provide the remote support service requested by the User. The Parties agree that, should the User fail to authorize the communication of their data to the Customer/Licensee, it will not be possible for the Customer/Licensee to provide the remote technical support service requested by the User.

The personal data communicated by the Users who download, install and use the APP will be processed by the Data Controller for the following purposes: To allow the Customer/Licensee to provide, through the app, the technical support requested by the end customer.

The personal data communicated by the Users who download, install and use the APP will be processed by the Data Controller for the following purposes: To enable the Data Controller to carry out operations that are closely connected and instrumental to the correct management of the relations with the Users, to the provision of the services requested by the above-mentioned Users, also about the functioning and correct use of the APP;

The personal data communicated by the Users who download, install and use the APP will be processed by the Data Controller for the following purposes: To perform statistical analyses in an anonymous and aggregated form;

The personal data communicated by the Users who download, install and use the APP will be processed by the Data Controller for the following purposes: To fulfill the obligations of the laws or regulations in force;

The personal data communicated by the Users who download, install and use the APP will be processed by the Data Controller for the following purposes: To manage any complaints and enable the Data Controller to protect its rights.
The User doesn’t need to confer their data when registering, but failure could prevent the Customer/Licensee from providing the requested support service.
Following registration, the User can revoke the consent granted by sending a simple written request addressed to the Data Controller.

The data will be processed lawfully, according to the principles of correctness, and in compliance with necessity, relevance, completeness, and minimization of the purposes for which they are processed.

The Data Controller shall process the data of the User mainly by electronic means suitable for guaranteeing, to the purposes for which the data have been collected, the safety and confidentiality of the same, and to prevent unauthorized access to the data for a period not exceeding that necessary for the purposes indicated above.

The Users’ data which has been submitted to ICONA when filling in the APP Registration form, will be kept by the Data Controller for a period not exceeding 5 (five) years from the date on which the APP is last used for the purposes described above, and subsequently for the time which Icona is subject to retention obligations for tax purposes, and any other purposes provided for by the law or regulations.

The Data Controller will store the data about the support activities (“Support History”) in Cloud mode managed by the Acty service for a period not exceeding 2 (two) years, also to enable statistical analyses to be performed in anonymous and aggregated form.

To pursue the purposes indicated above, the Data Controller may also need to communicate the personal data of the User to third parties belonging, by way of example only, to the following categories: parent and subsidiary companies; parties that perform technical and IT duties on behalf of ICONA; parties that provide services for the management of the information system of ICONA; parties that perform customer support activities (e.g., call centers, etc.); parties that perform filing and data entry activities; professional studios and companies in the field of consultancy and support relationships; parties that perform market research upon appointment from ICONA. The parties belonging to the categories reported above will process the Users’ data as Data Supervisors appointed specifically by the Data Controller. The list of the third parties to which the Data Controller will communicate the Users’ personal data is available for consultation by the User at the Data Controller’s registered office or by writing to the following email address: legal@icona.it.
The personal data processed for the above purposes are not subject to disclosure or a fully automated decision-making process.

The User, by downloading, installing, and using the APP, explicitly authorizes the Customer/Licensee companies of ICONA, who have purchased the user license for the ACTY software, to use the camera of their mobile device as a video camera only to provide the technical support requested from time to time via the APP.

The User, by downloading, installing, and using the APP, explicitly accepts that the video camera will be controlled remotely by the operator of the selected Customer, who will only have access to the video camera for the time necessary to provide the technical support or maintenance service requested by the User, and will have absolutely no possibility of accessing other functions or contents of the mobile device belonging to the User. The Customer/Licensee and their operator will only have access to photographs and video recordings made in compliance with and execution of the support or maintenance service requested by the User.
The express written request on the user’s part, the Customer/Licensee, may have access to the Gallery of the mobile device.

The User, by downloading, installing, and using the APP, explicitly authorizes ICONA, on behalf of the Customer/Licensee, to store, in cloud mode managed by the Acty service, the photographs and video recordings taken and made by the Customer’s operator during the support session and the photographs and video-recordings acquired (at the express request of the user) from the Gallery of the mobile device, to allow the Customer/Licensee to provide the service requested. Indeed, the Customer’s operator must create a personalized support form for the User containing photographs and video recordings.

ICONA can access the data stored on the Acty Cloud for the sole and exclusive purpose of archive maintenance and problem-solving. Access to Acty Cloud is limited to ICONA employees with a hardware access key provided by the company.

Concerning the purposes and procedures of the processing and the times for storing the User’s data by the Customer/Licensee, which provides the remote support service requested by the User, the above-mentioned Customer/Licensee, as an autonomous Data Controller, is responsible and obliged to provide the User with the privacy statement under Article 13 of Legislative Decree No 196 of 30 June 2003 and Article 13 of Regulation (EU) 679/2016.

Each User may exercise, by simple written request to be sent to the Data Controller, all rights conferred on it by the GDPR; by way of example but not limited to: The right of access to one’s data, to obtain confirmation of their existence care of the Data Controller, to know their origin and the purposes and procedures of the relative processing;

Each User may exercise, by simple written request to be sent to the Data Controller, all rights conferred on it by the GDPR; by way of example but not limited to: The right to know the data which identifies the Data Controller and any Data Supervisors and DPO, where appointed;

Each User may exercise, by simple written request to be sent to the Data Controller, all rights conferred on it by the GDPR; by way of example but not limited to: The right to know the parties or categories of parties to which the personal data have been communicated;

Each User may exercise, by simple written request to be sent to the Data Controller, all rights conferred on it by the GDPR; by way of example but not limited to: The right to obtain rectification of inaccurate data or the completion of incomplete data; deletion of personal data that concerns them (upon the occurrence of any of the conditions specified in art. 17, paragraph 1 of the GDPR and by the exceptions provided for in paragraph 3 of the same article); the limitation of the processing of their data (to have recourse to one of the hypotheses indicated in art. 18, paragraph 1 of the GDPR);

Each User may exercise, by simple written request to be sent to the Data Controller, all rights conferred on it by the GDPR; by way of example but not limited to: The right to request and obtain from the Data Controller – in cases in which the legal basis for the data processing is the agreement or the consent and the same is performed by automated means – one’s data in a structured and legible format by the automatic device, also to communicate these data to another Data Controller (the so-called right of portability of personal data);

Each User may exercise, by simple written request to be sent to the Data Controller, all rights conferred on it by the GDPR; by way of example but not limited to: The right to object to the processing of one’s data for legitimate reasons;

Each User may exercise, by simple written request to be sent to the Data Controller, all rights conferred on it by the GDPR; by way of example but not limited to: Requests must be sent in writing to the company ICON SRL at its unit located at Viale Brianza, 20 20092 Cinisello Balsamo (MI).
Cookies are small text files that the websites the user visits send to their browser, where they are temporarily memorized.
Regarding the types of cookies used by the company ICONA Srl on the www.acty.com site, and to acquire any additional information about these, full reference should be made to the Cookies Policy.
